key_insight: A variational nonlocal phase-field framework models dynamic fracture with weaker regularity requirements, no explicit crack tracking, and an emergent diffusive crack length set by the nonlocal interaction domain.
engineering_relevance: It provides a more flexible and variationally consistent tool for simulating crack initiation, branching, and fragmentation in elastic solids under dynamic loading.
method: Nonlocal kinematics and kernel-dependent function spaces recover local theory as the interaction domain shrinks.
A nonlocal crack-surface functional replaces the classical Ambrosio--Tortorelli regularization.
Coupled nonlocal momentum balance and irreversible phase-field gradient flow are solved with scalar auxiliary-variable and staggered time schemes plus finite elements.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports successful simulations of Mode-I fracture, dynamic branching, Kalthoff--Winkler-type shear fracture, and fragmentation, with crack-tip speeds below 0.6c_R and a shear crack angle of about 48°.
watch_item: Accuracy and computational cost may depend strongly on the chosen nonlocal kernel, interaction radius, and time-discretization strategy.
paper_title: A Variational Nonlocal Phase-Field Model for Dynamic Fracture in Elastic Solids
